seoTitle: Thuddungra vs Thumb Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Thuddungra vs Thumb Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thuddungra and Thumb Creek.
heading: Thuddungra vs Thumb Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thuddungra, New South Wales and Thumb Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thuddungra has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Thumb Creek (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thuddungra or Thumb Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thuddungra has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Thumb Creek scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thuddungra scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Thumb Creek scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thuddungra or Thumb Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thuddungra has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
